free_text: Angular and energy distributions of prompt neutrons
from thermal neutron-induced fission of U-233, U-235
reactor WWR-M  equipped with a neutron guide of 3m
determination of the times of flight of a neutron and
two fragments along with the angle between the velocity
vectors of neutron and light fragment.
Double discrimination by the pulse shape and
time-of-flight was used to separate the events produced
10 weekly cycles of measurement were performed.
One MWPD as start detector, located within
7mm range from target parallel to target plane.
Eight pairs of multiwire proportional fission-fragment
detectors, operating voltage 400-500 V, placed in two
arching formations diametrically opposed each other in
the reaction chamber at distance of 140mm from the
registration - stilbene crystals of 50 mm diameter,
50mm high and 60 mm diameter, 40mm high mounted on
angle between their respective axis at distance about
50 cm from target. Cylindrical shield made of 30 mm
thick layer of lead and 40mm thick layer of
150-200 keV. Position of neutron detectors was fixed.
Total counting rate was about 100 events/min for both
U-235 target, enriched to 99.99% purity, consisted of
280 microg/cm**2 thick U-235 layer sprayed as UF4 on
making a circular spot of 15 mm diameter.
measurements. Applied to the raw spectrum. As result,
the FWHM of the fragment-gamma-quantum coincidence
to neutron scattering and insufficient neutron-gamma
correction for efficiency differences of MWPDs,
correction for difference in registration efficiency
for light and heavy fragments by start detector (<=2%)
-Correction for the limited transparency of the start
detector is minimal and can be neglected.
distribution of fission fragments due to transmission
through the target backing and due to the neutron
Neutron spectra were normalized to the average fission
Data of each weekly cycle were processed separately.

Results obtained independently for two neutron
detectors were averaged and combined to produce

used to obtain neutron energy spectra for light and

used to obtain neutron energy and angular

Data of Fig.6 left, right of S,ISINN-17,60,2010
marked as "experimental" are not from this experiment.
The yield of 'scission' neutrons has been estimated in
the framework of a simple evaporation model and was
found to be less 5% of the total neutron yield.
The specific energy per nucleon for light and heavy

values.   Analysis of errors is not finished yet.

Cf and U time-of-flight spectra were obtained in
separate measurement but in the same experimental
Total prompt neutron spectra of Cf-252 and U-235 were
obtained independently for two neutron detectors as
weighted averages of 4 measurement cycles. Final result
is a weighted average of two ratios obtained by
Total correction factor applied to ratio Cf/U.
Uncertainty of normalization defined as the
difference between ratios obtained by two individual
a double discrimination method - time-of-flight and
time dependent background related with coincidence
between neutron and fission fragment which belong to a
different fission event. It was calculated analogous to
REL-REF of W.P.Poenitz+ and then it was subtracted;
the remaining part of background is the sum of
independent background, which results from random
coincidence, and background component, which increases
roughly linearly with time -the value of this component
was estimated from a count number at the discrimination
cut-off (En about 0.2 MeV). Separate experiments with a
shadow cone have been performed recently to determine
value and shape of the remaining part of background.
- Ratio was corrected for finite energy and angular
- Also the normalization correction connected to the
fact that in the measurement the experimental histogram
distributions instead of continuous distribution is
